text.title: db-chroma Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for db-chroma as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using in-memory chromadb.Client() in a pipeline that restarts between ingestion and retrieval.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: In-memory client loses all data on process exit. Use PersistentClient with a directory path.

failure-mode.description: Running concurrent writes from multiple processes against the same PersistentClient directory.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SQLite allows only one writer at a time; concurrent writes from separate processes corrupt the database.

failure-mode.description: Storing full document text as metadata values (kilobytes per document).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Chroma loads all metadata into memory; large payloads bloat RAM and slow queries. Use the documents field or store large content externally.
